name: Zed Context7 MCP Server
what_it_does: Zed Context7 MCP server enabling AI agents in the Zed code editor to access up-to-date library documentation via Context7 — fetching current API documentation for npm packages and libraries, retrieving framework guides, and integrating Context7's curated documentation into Zed's AI coding assistance workflows.
use_cases: Fetching current library API docs during coding from Zed AI agents
Accessing framework documentation without leaving the editor from development agents
Retrieving up-to-date npm package documentation from dependency guidance agents
Providing accurate library usage examples from code suggestion agents
Avoiding hallucinated API calls with real documentation from QA agents
Integrating Context7 docs into Zed coding assistant workflows
not_for: Non-Zed editors (Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code have their own Context7 configs)
Private internal library documentation (Context7 covers public packages only)
best_when: A developer using Zed editor needs accurate, current library documentation integrated into AI coding assistance — Context7 prevents hallucinated API calls.
avoid_when: You're using VS Code, Cursor, or other editors — each has its own Context7 MCP configuration. Or if you need private internal documentation.

auth.notes: Context7 free tier requires no authentication. Configuration in Zed settings to connect MCP server. Community configuration package.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Context7 free tier for public library documentation
pricing.paid_tiers: Context7 Pro for higher rate limits and additional features
pricing.notes: Context7 has a free tier. MCP configuration is free and open source. Community Zed-specific configuration.

performance.rate_limits: Context7 API rate limits apply on free tier

known_agent_gotchas: Zed-specific MCP configuration — not a general-purpose Context7 MCP
Community configuration from individual developer — not official Context7 or Zed
Context7 coverage varies by library — some packages have better docs than others
Rate limits on free tier may throttle heavy documentation queries
Zed MCP integration requires Zed version with MCP support
Context7 focuses on public npm/PyPI packages — internal libraries not covered
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced via Context7 API. No auth required for free tier. Community MCP config. No sensitive data sent — only library/package name queries.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. No auth required. Community config. Only library name queries sent externally.
